# Comprehensive ICD-9 and ICD-10 Code List for Major Adverse Cardiovascular Events (MACE)
## Primary Definition: Acute Myocardial Infarction + Ischemic Stroke + Hemorrhagic Stroke + Cardiovascular Death

**Document Date:** January 2, 2026  
**Scope:** EHR/Administrative Data Extraction - Parent Codes Only  
**Exclusions:** Post-MI complications, traumatic events, clinical suspicion codes  
**Position:** Primary diagnosis focus; first occurrence tracked
**Generated:** Generated by querying perplexity.ai

---

## 1. ACUTE MYOCARDIAL INFARCTION (AMI)

### ICD-9-CM Codes
| Code | Description | PPV | Notes |
|------|-------------|-----|-------|
| **410** | Acute myocardial infarction (all subcategories) | 95-96% | Most common code in literature (72% of ICD-9 MI studies). Includes all wall locations and STEMI/NSTEMI. Use parent code. |
| ~~412~~ | **EXCLUDE**: Old myocardial infarction | N/A | Not acute; used >8 weeks post-MI per guidelines. Outside MACE window. |

### ICD-10-CM Codes
| Code | Description | PPV | Notes |
|------|-------------|-----|-------|
| **I21** | Acute myocardial infarction (STEMI, NSTEMI, unspecified) | 80-85% | Parent code covers I21.0-I21.4, I21.9. Within 4-week (28-day) acute window. |
| **I22** | Subsequent myocardial infarction | 80-85% | For recurrent MI within 28 days of prior event. Include if tracking all acute MIs. |
| I21.A1 | Myocardial infarction type 2 (secondary cause) | Valid | *Borderline*: Consider including in main analysis; represents true MI despite secondary etiology. |
| I21.A9 | Other myocardial infarction type | Valid | *Borderline*: Include if capturing all acute MI types. |

### Summary
**Primary codes to use:**
- **ICD-9:** 410
- **ICD-10:** I21, I22
- **Do NOT include:** ICD-9 412; traumatic MI codes (e.g., codes with trauma prefix)

---

## 2. ISCHEMIC STROKE (Cerebral Infarction)

### ICD-9-CM Codes
| Code | Description | PPV (Primary) | PPV (Any Position) | Notes |
|------|-------------|--|--|-------|
| 433.x1 | Occlusion/stenosis precerebral arteries **with infarction** | 71% | ~50% | *Borderline*: Include if specificity critical. Fifth-digit "1" critical: .x0 (no infarction) only 13% PPV; exclude .x0. |
| 433.x0 | Occlusion/stenosis precerebral arteries **without infarction** | 13% | ~10% | **EXCLUDE** - too speculative; reflects coding uncertainty. |
| **434** | Occlusion of cerebral arteries | 90% | 85% | **INCLUDE**: Highest PPV of individual codes. Captures embolism (434.1) and thrombosis (434.0). |
| 434.x1 | ...with cerebral infarction | 85-90% | 85% | Preferred specificity if fifth-digit available. |
| 434.x0 | ...without cerebral infarction | ~30% | ~30% | *Borderline*: Low PPV; many are coded incorrectly. Exclude if possible. |
| **436** | Acute but ill-defined cerebrovascular disease | 80% | 72% | **INCLUDE**: PPV 72%, increased sensitivity over time. Captures cases where specifics unclear. |
| ~~435~~ | **EXCLUDE**: Transient cerebral ischemia (TIA) | ~10% | ~10% | TIA by definition <24h, not acute stroke. Meets user exclusion criteria. |
| ~~437~~ | **EXCLUDE**: Other ill-defined cerebrovascular disease | ~8-15% | ~8-15% | Too non-specific; chronic/long-term codes. |
| ~~438~~ | **EXCLUDE**: Late effects of cerebrovascular disease | ~2% | ~2% | Sequelae code, not acute event. Very low PPV. |

### ICD-10-CM Codes
| Code | Description | PPV (Estimated) | Notes |
|------|-------------|--|-------|
| **I63** | Cerebral infarction (all subtypes) | 85-90% | **INCLUDE**: Parent code covers all ischemic stroke pathways (thrombosis, embolism, unspecified occlusion). |
| I63.0-I63.6 | Specific etiology (thrombosis, embolism, venous, etc.) | 85-90% | Can use parent I63 or specific subcodes; validation similar. |
| I63.8 | Other cerebral infarction | Valid | Include; captures documented ischemic strokes with unusual features. |
| I63.9 | Cerebral infarction, unspecified | Valid | Include; analogous to ICD-9 436. |
| I65, I66 | Occlusion/stenosis of cerebral arteries (not resulting in infarction) | Lower | *Borderline*: Some studies include as ischemic stroke proxy; many exclude. Recommend EXCLUDE if I63 available. |
| ~~I64~~ | **EXCLUDE**: Stroke, not specified as hemorrhage or infarction | ~50-60% (low) | Too non-specific; reflects coding/documentation uncertainty per guidelines. |
| ~~G45~~ | **EXCLUDE**: Transient ischemic attack | N/A | TIA, not stroke. <24h definition. |

### Summary
**Recommended ICD-9-CM Codes:**
- **Core:** 434, 436
- **Optional (higher specificity):** 433.x1 (with infarction modifier only; EXCLUDE 433.x0)
- **Combined algorithm:** 434 + 436 (PPV ~85-90% when used as primary diagnosis)

**Recommended ICD-10-CM Codes:**
- **Core:** I63 (parent code captures all acute ischemic stroke subtypes)
- **Exclude:** I64 (stroke not specified), G45 (TIA)

---

## 3. HEMORRHAGIC STROKE

### Subarachnoid Hemorrhage (SAH)

**ICD-9-CM:**
| Code | Description | PPV | Notes |
|------|-------------|-----|-------|
| **430** | Subarachnoid hemorrhage | 79% | INCLUDE: Highest specificity for SAH. |

**ICD-10-CM:**
| Code | Description | PPV | Notes |
|------|-------------|-----|-------|
| **I60** | Subarachnoid hemorrhage (nontraumatic) | ~88-93% | INCLUDE: Parent code; specify artery if possible (I60.0-I60.9) but parent acceptable. |
| I60.0-I60.9 | Specific artery involvement | ~88-93% | Can use parent I60 or specific subcodes. |

### Intracerebral Hemorrhage (ICH)

**ICD-9-CM:**
| Code | Description | PPV | Notes |
|------|-------------|-----|-------|
| **431** | Intracerebral hemorrhage | 69% | INCLUDE: Moderate PPV; good specificity. Includes all locations (hemisphere, brainstem, cerebellum). |
| ~~432~~ | **EXCLUDE**: Other/unspecified intracranial hemorrhage | 8% | **CRITICALLY LOW PPV (8%)**. ARIC study: only 7 true strokes vs. 83 false positives. Adds substantial false positive burden. Recommend strong exclusion. |

**ICD-10-CM:**
| Code | Description | PPV | Notes |
|------|-------------|-----|-------|
| **I61** | Intracerebral hemorrhage (nontraumatic) | 85-90% | INCLUDE: Parent code covers all locations (I61.0-I61.6, I61.8-I61.9). Higher PPV than ICD-9 431. |
| I61.0-I61.9 | Specific location variants | 85-90% | Can specify by location (hemisphere, brainstem, cerebellum, intraventricular, multiple) or use parent I61. |

### Other Intracranial Hemorrhage (Subdural, Epidural)

**ICD-9-CM:**
| Code | Description | Recommendation | Notes |
|------|-------------|--|-------|
| ~~432.0~~ | Nontraumatic extradural hemorrhage | EXCLUDE | Part of low-PPV 432 category. |
| ~~432.1~~ | Subdural hemorrhage | **CONSIDER EXCLUDING** | Nontraumatic subdural hemorrhage; not typically part of acute MACE definition, often chronic. Validation data limited. |

**ICD-10-CM:**
| Code | Description | Recommendation | Notes |
|------|-------------|--|-------|
| ~~I62~~ | Other nontraumatic intracranial hemorrhage | **EXCLUDE** | Includes subdural (I62.0), epidural (I62.1), unspecified (I62.9). Not typically core MACE component; chronic/traumatic risk. Validation shows ~88% PPV but may represent different phenotype. |

### Summary
**Recommended Hemorrhagic Stroke Codes:**

**ICD-9-CM (High Specificity):**
- **SAH:** 430
- **ICH:** 431
- **EXCLUDE:** 432 (too low PPV)

**ICD-10-CM (High Specificity):**
- **SAH:** I60
- **ICH:** I61
- **EXCLUDE:** I62 (not typical MACE component)

---

## 4. CARDIOVASCULAR DEATH

*Note: CV death coding in EHR is heterogeneous and often relies on death certificate/vital records rather than diagnostic codes.*

### ICD-9-CM Codes
*Limited standardization.* Some approaches:
- **Cardiac arrest:** 427.5 (ventricular fibrillation related)
- **Cardiogenic shock:** 785.51 (often a mechanism, not primary outcome)
- **Broad codes:** Range 410-414, 420-427, 428-429 (all cardiovascular)

**Recommendation:** Use death registry with underlying cause ICD codes (I00-I99 range) rather than discharge diagnosis codes for accuracy.

### ICD-10-CM Codes
| Code | Description | Notes |
|------|-------------|-------|
| **I46** | Cardiac arrest | **PREFERRED** for sudden CV death. |
| I46.0 | Cardiac arrest with successful resuscitation | Survived event; may not represent mortality. Consider context. |
| I46.1 | Sudden cardiac death, so described | **PRIMARY CODE** for documented SCD. |
| I46.2 | Cardiac arrest due to underlying cardiac condition | Any cardiac condition triggering arrest. |
| I46.8 | Cardiac arrest due to other underlying condition | Captures non-primary cardiac causes. |
| I46.9 | Cardiac arrest, cause unspecified | Includes unspecified sudden cardiac death. |

**Alternative approach:** Use I00-I99 (all circulatory disease codes) to capture broader CV mortality from death registries.

### Summary
**Recommended CV Death Codes:**
- **Preferred in EHR:** ICD-10 I46 (cardiac arrest codes), particularly I46.1, I46.9
- **Alternative:** Link to death registry using underlying cause ICD codes (I00-I99)
- **Note:** CV death is inconsistently coded in EHR; validation with vital records/death certificates recommended.

---

## COMPREHENSIVE MACE CODE LIST (SUMMARY TABLE)

### ICD-9-CM
| Component | Core Codes | Alternative/Borderline | Exclude |
|-----------|-----------|----------------------|---------|
| **AMI** | 410 | None | 412 |
| **Ischemic Stroke** | 434, 436 | 433.x1* | 433.x0, 435, 437, 438 |
| **Hemorrhagic Stroke** | 430, 431 | None | 432 |
| **CV Death** | See I46 (ICD-10) or death registry | 427.5, 785.51 | None specific |

*433.x1 = with infarction modifier only; exclude 433.x0 (without infarction)

### ICD-10-CM
| Component | Core Codes | Alternative/Borderline | Exclude |
|-----------|-----------|----------------------|---------|
| **AMI** | I21, I22 | I21.A1, I21.A9 | None |
| **Ischemic Stroke** | I63 | I65, I66 | I64, G45 |
| **Hemorrhagic Stroke** | I60, I61 | None | I62 |
| **CV Death** | I46 (esp. I46.1, I46.9) | I00-I99 (death registry) | None |

---

## FLAGGED ITEMS FOR USER DECISION

1. **ICD-9 433 (Precerebral occlusion):**
   - **Status:** Borderline
   - **Issue:** Very low PPV overall (10%), but ICD-9 433.x1 (with infarction modifier) ~71% PPV
   - **Recommendation:** Include 433.x1 only if fifth-digit coding available; EXCLUDE 433.x0
   - **Alternative:** Use 434 + 436 combination (simpler, high PPV)

2. **ICD-9 432 (Other intracranial hemorrhage):**
   - **Status:** RECOMMEND EXCLUDE
   - **Issue:** Only 8% PPV in ARIC validation study (7 true vs 83 false positives)
   - **Impact:** Adds substantial false-positive hemorrhagic stroke cases
   - **Rationale:** Poor specificity outweighs coverage

3. **ICD-9 436 (Acute but ill-defined cerebrovascular disease):**
   - **Status:** RECOMMEND INCLUDE
   - **Issue:** Reflects incomplete documentation/specification
   - **Data:** PPV 72%, sensitivity increased over time (1987-2010: 24% → 93%)
   - **Rationale:** Included in FDA/EMA guidance; captures real events with coding limitations

4. **ICD-10 I64 (Stroke not specified as hemorrhage or infarction):**
   - **Status:** RECOMMEND EXCLUDE
   - **Issue:** Too non-specific; reflects coding uncertainty/missing workup
   - **Rationale:** Should be classifiable as I63 or I60/I61 with proper documentation

5. **ICD-10 I62 (Other intracranial hemorrhage, including subdural):**
   - **Status:** BORDERLINE; Recommend EXCLUDE from core MACE
   - **Issue:** Primarily chronic subdural hemorrhage; not typical acute MACE component
   - **Rationale:** Different phenotype (often trauma-related, older population); validation data differ from acute SAH/ICH

6. **ICD-10 I21.A1, I21.A9 (Type 2 and other MI):**
   - **Status:** BORDERLINE; Recommend INCLUDE in sensitivity analysis
   - **Issue:** Secondary/demand ischemia (e.g., sepsis, anemia triggering infarction); still true MI
   - **Rationale:** Include in main or sensitivity analysis depending on clinical question

7. **Cardiovascular Death Operationalization:**
   - **Status:** REQUIRES USER INPUT
   - **Options:**
     - Option A: ICD-10 I46 (cardiac arrest codes from EHR discharge diagnoses)
     - Option B: Link to death registry/vital records using underlying cause codes (I00-I99)
     - Option C: Hybrid: I46 + death registry for completeness
   - **Recommendation:** Option B or C preferred; I46 alone may miss non-arrest CV deaths (MI, stroke) recorded as primary cause

---

## VALIDATION SUMMARY & QUALITY METRICS

| Component | ICD-9 | ICD-10 | Data Source | Sample Size |
|-----------|-------|--------|-------------|------------|
| **AMI (410)** | 95-96% PPV | 80-85% PPV | Multiple validation studies | >4000 hospitalizations |
| **Ischemic Stroke (434/436)** | 75-90% PPV (434); 72% (436) | 85-90% PPV (I63) | ARIC Study, Andrade systematic review | 4260+ hospitalizations |
| **SAH (430/I60)** | 79% PPV | 88-93% PPV | ARIC, multiple studies | Smaller sample size |
| **ICH (431/I61)** | 69% PPV | 85-90% PPV | ARIC, multiple studies | Smaller sample size |

**Recommendation:** Use parent codes where possible. Codes in primary diagnosis position have ~5-10% higher PPV than any-position coding.

---

## USAGE NOTES FOR EHR EXTRACTION

1. **Time Window:**
   - **ICD-9:** AMI/Stroke codes valid within 4 weeks (28 days); 412 not valid for acute MACE
   - **ICD-10:** AMI codes (I21, I22) valid within 28 days

2. **Diagnosis Position:**
   - Prioritize primary diagnosis if available
   - Any-position codes acceptable but with ~5-10% lower PPV

3. **Trauma/Iatrogenic Exclusions:**
   - Exclude codes with trauma prefix (800-899 ICD-9; V/W codes ICD-10)
   - Exclude iatrogenic codes (997.02 ICD-9, I97.x ICD-10)

4. **Recurrent Events:**
   - ICD-9: Track 410 vs. 412 sequencing and dates
   - ICD-10: Use I22 to identify subsequent MI within 28 days of prior I21

5. **Validation Recommendation:**
   - 67% of observational MACE studies do NOT validate codes
   - **Strongly recommend:** Validate algorithm on subset of charts (n=50-100) for your population
   - Perform sensitivity analyses excluding borderline codes

---

## REFERENCES & DATA SOURCES

- Bosco et al. (2021): Systematic review of MACE definitions in 58 observational studies [PMC8571870]
- Rosamond et al. (2014, ARIC Study): Validity of ICD-9-CM stroke codes across 4260 hospitalizations
- Andrade et al. (2012): Systematic review of validated CVA/TIA algorithms (35 studies, 1990-2010)
- Birman-Deych et al. (2005): ICD-9-CM code accuracy for cardiovascular risk factors
- FDA/EMA Guidance (2012): Three-point MACE definition (AMI + stroke + CV death)

---

## FINAL RECOMMENDATIONS

### Conservative Definition (High Specificity, ~85% PPV)
```
ICD-9:  410 + (434 + 436) + (430 + 431)  + I46 (ICD-10)
ICD-10: I21 + I22 + I63 + I60 + I61 + I46
```

### Standard Definition (Balanced Sensitivity/Specificity, ~80% PPV)
```
ICD-9:  410 + (433.x1 + 434 + 436) + (430 + 431) + I46 (ICD-10)
ICD-10: I21 + I22 + I63 + I60 + I61 + I46
```

### Broad Definition (Higher Sensitivity, ~75% PPV)
```
ICD-9:  410 + (433 + 434 + 436) + (430 + 431 + 432) + I46 (ICD-10)
ICD-10: I21 + I22 + I63 + I64 + I60 + I61 + I62 + I46
```

**User Note:** Conservative definition recommended for primary analysis. Use broad definition for sensitivity analysis to assess impact of borderline codes.
